payload.intent: Make a dropped MCP connection INVISIBLE to the client: on reconnect, re-attach to the in-flight run and catch up on everything missed, instead of restarting. This is durable-substrate S5, the transport finisher; it consumes S1 read_events_since, S2 idempotent submit / re-attach, and S3a durable terminal outcome. Use app-level resync over existing stdio MCP tools unless implementation evidence forces otherwise. Scope: 1. Add a reconnect/catch-up path: given run_id and last_event_id, return all events since via State.read_events_since; clients advance their cursor exactly once, ascending and gap-tolerant. 2. Re-attach: reconnecting client re-submits idempotently and gets the SAME job back with no duplicate run; poll returns terminal outcome from the ledger if completed during disconnect. 3. Document the reconnect protocol: client persists run_id and last_event_id; on drop -> reconnect -> idempotent re-submit -> catch_up(last_event_id) -> resume polling. 4. Do not rewrite transport or build Streamable HTTP/EventStore in this slice. Non-goals: no S3b projection rebuild, no gate/reviewer changes, no transport rewrite. Acceptance: simulated drop mid-run -> reconnect -> client catches up on all missed events exactly once, re-attaches to same job with no duplicate spawn, and reads terminal outcome from ledger if completed during disconnect; reconnect protocol is covered by an end-to-end test and documented; deterministic replay preserved; full suite green; ledger and replay artifacts exported.

Supervisor-owned workflow gate: prd_review.

Intent:
Make a dropped MCP connection INVISIBLE to the client: on reconnect, re-attach to the in-flight run and catch up on everything missed, instead of restarting. This is durable-substrate S5, the transport finisher; it consumes S1 read_events_since, S2 idempotent submit / re-attach, and S3a durable terminal outcome. Use app-level resync over existing stdio MCP tools unless implementation evidence forces otherwise. Scope: 1. Add a reconnect/catch-up path: given run_id and last_event_id, return all events since via State.read_events_since; clients advance their cursor exactly once, ascending and gap-tolerant. 2. Re-attach: reconnecting client re-submits idempotently and gets the SAME job back with no duplicate run; poll returns terminal outcome from the ledger if completed during disconnect. 3. Document the reconnect protocol: client persists run_id and last_event_id; on drop -> reconnect -> idempotent re-submit -> catch_up(last_event_id) -> resume polling. 4. Do not rewrite transport or build Streamable HTTP/EventStore in this slice. Non-goals: no S3b projection rebuild, no gate/reviewer changes, no transport rewrite. Acceptance: simulated drop mid-run -> reconnect -> client catches up on all missed events exactly once, re-attaches to same job with no duplicate spawn, and reads terminal outcome from ledger if completed during disconnect; reconnect protocol is covered by an end-to-end test and documented; deterministic replay preserved; full suite green; ledger and replay artifacts exported.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: S1 read_events_since exists exactly as the PRD describes
S2 idempotent submit/reattach returns same job with no duplicate worker
S3a poll prefers ledger terminal outcome over result.json
PRD scope excludes transport rewrite, Streamable HTTP, EventStore, S3b, and gate/reviewer changes
All six PRD grill findings marked resolved and consistent with PRD text

criteria: Dependency primitives S1/S2/S3a verified present in source: yes
PRD promises have public boundaries and forbidden outcomes: yes
Grill findings resolved and consistent: yes
Edge cases fully closed in artifacts: partial (two advisories)
Implementation/tests executed: no, not applicable at prd_review
evidence: ACCEPT: the prd_review gate should advance
Advisory (non-blocking): fold cursor-persistence ordering rule into Slice 3 protocol doc so exactly-once survives client crash mid-page
Advisory (non-blocking): protocol doc should make client_token effectively mandatory on reconnect to guarantee P2 reattach
confidence.rationale: Every dependency the PRD relies on was verified directly in current source and matches the PRD's evidence claims, and the PRD is well-formed with bounded promises and explicit non-goals. Confidence held below 0.9 because this is a planning artifact whose two real edge cases (client-side exactly-once durability and idempotency-token stability under payload drift) are not yet pinned down in the protocol doc or e2e test, and no implementation or test execution exists at this gate.

payload.content: PRD for S5 resumable transport reviewed against source. All consumed primitives (S1 read_events_since + (run_id,event_id) index, S2 idempotent reattach with no duplicate spawn, S3a ledger-first poll) verified factually present in current code. Six grill findings resolved; promises P1-P5 have clear boundaries and forbidden outcomes; out-of-scope explicit. Recommend ACCEPT with two advisory clarifications for the protocol doc and e2e test.

assumptions_to_verify: Clients in practice will always pass a stable client_token on reconnect (PRD allows the weaker 'same logical request')
Client persists next_event_id durably relative to event processing to achieve true exactly-once
Empty-page-preserves-cursor and has_more=count==limit paging behavior will be covered by the e2e test
contradictions_checked: PRD claim that read_events_since uses event_id>? ASC with (run_id,event_id) index -- confirmed true at state.py:451,67
PRD claim that reattach launches no second worker -- confirmed: created=False branch returns before Popen at stdio.py:1800
PRD claim that poll prefers ledger over result.json -- confirmed: terminal_outcome_json read first at stdio.py:1923
PRD claim that catch_up tool does not yet exist -- confirmed absent, consistent with it being net-new
missing_evidence: No implementation or test run exists yet (expected at prd_review, but means promises are unproven end-to-end)
No draft of the P4 reconnect protocol doc to confirm it will state cursor-persistence ordering and mandatory client_token
No confirmation that the e2e test (Slice 2) will assert the launcher is called exactly once across a varied-payload re-submit, not just an identical one
critical_review.strongest_objection: P2's 'no duplicate worker' promise depends on a stable idempotency token; since _workflow_job_idempotency_token derives from payload when client_token is omitted, a reconnecting client that varies any payload field without a client_token will spawn a duplicate worker, silently violating the promise unless the protocol doc forces client_token use.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Evidence that the reattach idempotency token can diverge under normal reconnect payloads with no client_token AND that the protocol doc does not require client_token would downgrade this from accept to revise; conversely a drafted protocol doc closing both advisories would push confidence above 0.9.

S3a poll prefers ledger terminal outcome over result.json

All six PRD grill findings marked resolved and consistent with PRD text

decisions: ACCEPT: the prd_review gate should advance
Advisory (non-blocking): fold cursor-persistence ordering rule into Slice 3 protocol doc so exactly-once survives client crash mid-page
Advisory (non-blocking): protocol doc should make client_token effectively mandatory on reconnect to guarantee P2 reattach

Supervisor-owned workflow gate: issues_review.

Intent:

Document the reconnect protocol: client persists run_id and last_event_id; on drop -> reconnect -> idempotent re-submit -> catch_up(last_event_id) -> resume polling. 4. catch_up tool confirmed not yet implemented, so this is a valid pre-implementation issues gate
confidence.rationale: Dependency primitives S1/S2/S3a were directly verified in source (read/grep) and match PRD claims; all PRD promises map to slices; no contradictions found across issues/PRD/TDD. payload.content: Issues decomposition for S5 resumable transport is sound and should advance. Four P0/P1 slices (catch-up tool, end-to-end reconnect, protocol doc, regression guard) cover all five PRD promises P1-P5. Dependency primitives S1 read_events_since, S2 idempotent submit/reattach, and S3a durable terminal outcome were verified to exist in source exactly as the PRD claims, so the app-level resync choice over a transport rewrite is evidence-based. catch_up_dual_agent_workflow is absent from source, confirming a genuine pre-implementation gate. Two minor advisory gaps: no explicit redaction assertion and no tool-boundary gap-tolerance assertion in slice ACs.

assumptions_to_verify: read_events_since output is safe/redacted for direct client return or the catch-up tool will apply redaction
existing poll_dual_agent_workflow_job already reads terminal outcome from the ledger when result.json is absent
S2 second-submit returns the original job_id under the exact token-derivation path the e2e test will exercise
contradictions_checked: Issues slice-to-PRD-promise mapping vs PRD promise list: consistent
PRD app-level resync decision vs actual server entrypoint and S1/S2/S3a presence: consistent with source
Non-goals (no transport rewrite/S3b/gate change) vs slice scopes: consistent
TDD test list vs issues acceptance criteria: consistent
missing_evidence: Execution of the focused or full pytest suite (not required at issues stage but would raise confidence in dependency behavior)
Confirmation that the existing poll tool already prefers the ledger over result.json at the tool boundary, not only that terminal columns exist
An explicit redaction guarantee on the events returned by read_events_since
critical_review.strongest_objection: As an artifact-only gate, acceptance rests on the issues mapping plus source verification of dependencies, not on executed implementation or tests; if a dependency primitive behaved differently than its signature suggests (e.g., read_events_since redaction or terminal-poll ledger preference not actually wired into the poll tool), a slice could be under-specified.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Evidence that a depended-on primitive does not behave as the PRD claims (e.g., poll does not actually fall back to the ledger, or read_events_since returns unredacted sensitive payloads with no redaction layer planned), or that a PRD promise has no covering slice, would move this from accept to revise.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: tdd_review.

Intent:
Make a dropped MCP connection INVISIBLE to the client: on reconnect, re-attach to the in-flight run and catch up on everything missed, instead of restarting. This is durable-substrate S5, the transport finisher; it consumes S1 read_events_since, S2 idempotent submit / re-attach, and S3a durable terminal outcome. Use app-level resync over existing stdio MCP tools unless implementation evidence forces otherwise. Scope: 1. Add a reconnect/catch-up path: given run_id and last_event_id, return all events since via State.read_events_since; clients advance their cursor exactly once, ascending and gap-tolerant. 2. Re-attach: reconnecting client re-submits idempotently and gets the SAME job back with no duplicate run; poll returns terminal outcome from the ledger if completed during disconnect. 3. Document the reconnect protocol: client persists run_id and last_event_id; on drop -> reconnect -> idempotent re-submit -> catch_up(last_event_id) -> resume polling. 4. Do not rewrite transport or build Streamable HTTP/EventStore in this slice. Non-goals: no S3b projection rebuild, no gate/reviewer changes, no transport rewrite. Acceptance: simulated drop mid-run -> reconnect -> client catches up on all missed events exactly once, re-attaches to same job with no duplicate spawn, and reads terminal outcome from ledger if completed during disconnect; reconnect protocol is covered by an end-to-end test and documented; deterministic replay preserved; full suite green; ledger and replay artifacts exported.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

End-to-end test composes S1+S2+S3a with launcher-once and no-duplicate second read.
Ledger terminal outcome is asserted after result.json deletion.
Reconnect protocol has a doc-presence test and a regression guard.

Require during GREEN: add an assertion that a catch_up call does not write any event (read-only proof of PRD P1 forbidden outcome).

Confidence is held below 0.9 because the full suite was not executed in this gate (test_status unknown) and two named acceptance facets (gap-tolerance, read-only) are not yet test-enforced.

payload.content: TDD plan for S5 resumable transport reviewed against live source. decisions: accept
Advance tdd_review: plan meets RED-at-public-boundary, e2e S1+S2+S3a composition, exactly-once via second catch-up, launcher-once, and ledger poll after result.json deletion.
Require during GREEN: public-boundary test seeding non-contiguous event_ids (PRD P1 gap-tolerant / forbidden contiguous assumption).
Require during GREEN: assert catch_up performs zero event writes (PRD P1 read-only forbidden outcome).
objections: Intent acceptance 'ascending and gap-tolerant' is not enforced by any planned public-boundary test; gap behavior is only documented (Slice 3) and covered at State layer by test_read_events_since_tolerates_non_contiguous_event_ids, not via catch_up.
PRD P1 forbids mutating the event stream during catch-up, but no planned test asserts unchanged event count after catch_up.
PRD has_more paging (has_more = count == limit) is not named in TDD GREEN criteria for the cursor-page test.
outcome.summary: Independent tdd_review: TDD plan is RED-first at catch_up_dual_agent_workflow, composes S1+S2+S3a in one drop/reconnect e2e test with launcher-once and second-catch-up no-duplicates, and maps to all five PRD promises. Verified S1 read_events_since (state.py:451), S2 reattach-before-Popen (stdio.py:1800), S3a ledger-first poll (stdio.py:1926), catch_up absent from implementation, and 98 focused regression tests passed. Accept with GREEN advisories for gap-tolerance and read-only assertions at the public boundary.

Verified S1 read_events_since (state.py:451), S2 reattach-before-Popen (stdio.py:1800), S3a ledger-first poll (stdio.py:1926), catch_up absent from implementation, and 98 focused regression tests passed. Accept with GREEN advisories for gap-tolerance and read-only assertions at the public boundary.

payload.intent: Make a dropped MCP connection INVISIBLE to the client: on reconnect, re-attach to the in-flight run and catch up on everything missed, instead of restarting. This is durable-substrate S5, the transport finisher; it consumes S1 read_events_since, S2 idempotent submit / re-attach, and S3a durable terminal outcome. Use app-level resync over existing stdio MCP tools unless implementation evidence forces otherwise. Scope: 1. Add a reconnect/catch-up path: given run_id and last_event_id, return all events since via State.read_events_since; clients advance their cursor exactly once, ascending and gap-tolerant. 2. Re-attach: reconnecting client re-submits idempotently and gets the SAME job back with no duplicate run; poll returns terminal outcome from the ledger if completed during disconnect. 3. Document the reconnect protocol: client persists run_id and last_event_id; on drop -> reconnect -> idempotent re-submit -> catch_up(last_event_id) -> resume polling. 4. Do not rewrite transport or build Streamable HTTP/EventStore in this slice. Non-goals: no S3b projection rebuild, no gate/reviewer changes, no transport rewrite. Acceptance: simulated drop mid-run -> reconnect -> client catches up on all missed events exactly once, re-attaches to same job with no duplicate spawn, and reads terminal outcome from ledger if completed during disconnect; reconnect protocol is covered by an end-to-end test and documented; deterministic replay preserved; full suite green; ledger and replay artifacts exported.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: implementation_plan.

Intent:
Make a dropped MCP connection invisible to the client: on reconnect, re-attach to the in-flight run and catch up on everything missed, instead of restarting. This is durable-substrate S5, the transport finisher; it consumes S1 read_events_since, S2 idempotent submit / re-attach, and S3a durable terminal outcome. Use app-level resync over existing stdio MCP tools unless implementation evidence forces otherwise. Scope: 1. Add a reconnect/catch-up path: given run_id and last_event_id, return all events since via State.read_events_since; clients advance their cursor exactly once, ascending and gap-tolerant. 2. Re-attach: reconnecting client re-submits idempotently and gets the SAME job back with no duplicate run; poll returns terminal outcome from the ledger if completed during disconnect. 3. Document the reconnect protocol: client persists run_id and last_event_id; on drop -> reconnect -> idempotent re-submit -> catch_up(last_event_id) -> resume polling. 4. Do not rewrite transport or build Streamable HTTP/EventStore in this slice. Non-goals: no S3b projection rebuild, no gate/reviewer changes, no transport rewrite. Acceptance: simulated drop mid-run -> reconnect -> client catches up on all missed events exactly once, re-attaches to same job with no duplicate spawn, and reads terminal outcome from ledger if completed during disconnect; reconnect protocol is covered by an integration test and documented; deterministic replay preserved; full suite green; ledger and replay artifacts exported.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Catch-up is read-only by design and does not write a catch-up event

All cited dependencies verified to exist with matching contracts. ACCEPT to advance to execution; minor non-blocking guardrails noted.

payload.content:
Independently review the implementation_plan gate for this tri-agent workflow.
Accept only if the gate should advance after reading the artifacts and Claude outcome.
Critical review: before accepting, try to disprove the Claude outcome and gate evidence. Identify the strongest reason this should not advance, missing evidence, contradictions checked, assumptions to verify, and what would change your decision. Keep this concise and auditable; do not include private chain-of-thought.
Return the structured critical_review object in the typed outcome.

Intent:
Make a dropped MCP connection invisible to the client: on reconnect, re-attach to the in-flight run and catch up on everything missed, instead of restarting. This is durable-substrate S5, the transport finisher; it consumes S1 read_events_since, S2 idempotent submit / re-attach, and S3a durable terminal outcome. Use app-level resync over existing stdio MCP tools unless implementation evidence forces otherwise. Scope: 1. Add a reconnect/catch-up path: given run_id and last_event_id, return all events since via State.read_events_since; clients advance their cursor exactly once, ascending and gap-tolerant. 2. Re-attach: reconnecting client re-submits idempotently and gets the SAME job back with no duplicate run; poll returns terminal outcome from the ledger if completed during disconnect. 3. Document the reconnect protocol: client persists run_id and last_event_id; on drop -> reconnect -> idempotent re-submit -> catch_up(last_event_id) -> resume polling. 4. Do not rewrite transport or build Streamable HTTP/EventStore in this slice. Non-goals: no S3b projection rebuild, no gate/reviewer changes, no transport rewrite. Acceptance: simulated drop mid-run -> reconnect -> client catches up on all missed events exactly once, re-attaches to same job with no duplicate spawn, and reads terminal outcome from ledger if completed during disconnect; reconnect protocol is covered by an integration test and documented; deterministic replay preserved; full suite green; ledger and replay artifacts exported.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: execution.

Intent:
Make a dropped MCP connection invisible to the client: on reconnect, re-attach to the in-flight run and catch up on everything missed, instead of restarting. This is durable-substrate S5, the transport finisher; it consumes S1 read_events_since, S2 idempotent submit / re-attach, and S3a durable terminal outcome. Use app-level resync over existing stdio MCP tools unless implementation evidence forces otherwise. Scope: 1. Add a reconnect/catch-up path: given run_id and last_event_id, return all events since via State.read_events_since; clients advance their cursor exactly once, ascending and gap-tolerant. 2. payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: execution.

Intent:
Make a dropped MCP connection invisible to the client: on reconnect, re-attach to the in-flight run and catch up on everything missed, instead of restarting. This is durable-substrate S5, the transport finisher; it consumes S1 read_events_since, S2 idempotent submit / re-attach, and S3a durable terminal outcome. Use app-level resync over existing stdio MCP tools unless implementation evidence forces otherwise. Scope: 1. Add a reconnect/catch-up path: given run_id and last_event_id, return all events since via State.read_events_since; clients advance their cursor exactly once, ascending and gap-tolerant. 2. Re-attach: reconnecting client re-submits idempotently and gets the SAME job back with no duplicate run; poll returns terminal outcome from the ledger if completed during disconnect. 3. Document the reconnect protocol: client persists run_id and last_event_id; on drop -> reconnect -> idempotent re-submit -> catch_up(last_event_id) -> resume polling. 4. Do not rewrite transport or build Streamable HTTP/EventStore in this slice. Non-goals: no S3b projection rebuild, no gate/reviewer changes, no transport rewrite. Acceptance: simulated drop mid-run -> reconnect -> client catches up on all missed events exactly once, re-attaches to same job with no duplicate spawn, and reads terminal outcome from ledger if completed during disconnect; reconnect protocol is covered by an integration test and documented; deterministic replay preserved; full suite green; ledger and replay artifacts exported.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: execution.

Intent:
Make a dropped MCP connection invisible to the client: on reconnect, re-attach to the in-flight run and catch up on everything missed, instead of restarting. This is durable-substrate S5, the transport finisher; it consumes S1 read_events_since, S2 idempotent submit / re-attach, and S3a durable terminal outcome. Use app-level resync over existing stdio MCP tools unless implementation evidence forces otherwise. Scope: 1. Add a reconnect/catch-up path: given run_id and last_event_id, return all events since via State.read_events_since; clients advance their cursor exactly once, ascending and gap-tolerant. 2. Re-attach: reconnecting client re-submits idempotently and gets the SAME job back with no duplicate run; poll returns terminal outcome from the ledger if completed during disconnect. 3. Document the reconnect protocol: client persists run_id and last_event_id; on drop -> reconnect -> idempotent re-submit -> catch_up(last_event_id) -> resume polling. 4. Do not rewrite transport or build Streamable HTTP/EventStore in this slice. Non-goals: no S3b projection rebuild, no gate/reviewer changes, no transport rewrite. Acceptance: simulated drop mid-run -> reconnect -> client catches up on all missed events exactly once, re-attaches to same job with no duplicate spawn, and reads terminal outcome from ledger if completed during disconnect; reconnect protocol is covered by an integration test and documented; deterministic replay preserved; full suite green; ledger and replay artifacts exported.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Corrective context from the previous round:

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: execution.

Intent:
Make a dropped MCP connection invisible to the client: on reconnect, re-attach to the in-flight run and catch up on everything missed, instead of restarting. This is durable-substrate S5, the transport finisher; it consumes S1 read_events_since, S2 idempotent submit / re-attach, and S3a durable terminal outcome. Use app-level resync over existing stdio MCP tools unless implementation evidence forces otherwise. Scope: 1. Add a reconnect/catch-up path: given run_id and last_event_id, return all events since via State.read_events_since; clients advance their cursor exactly once, ascending and gap-tolerant. 2. Re-attach: reconnecting client re-submits idempotently and gets the SAME job back with no duplicate run; poll returns terminal outcome from the ledger if completed during disconnect. 3. Document the reconnect protocol: client persists run_id and last_event_id; on drop -> reconnect -> idempotent re-submit -> catch_up(last_event_id) -> resume polling. 4. Do not rewrite transport or build Streamable HTTP/EventStore in this slice. Non-goals: no S3b projection rebuild, no gate/reviewer changes, no transport rewrite. Acceptance: simulated drop mid-run -> reconnect -> client catches up on all missed events exactly once, re-attaches to same job with no duplicate spawn, and reads terminal outcome from ledger if completed during disconnect; reconnect protocol is covered by an integration test and documented; deterministic replay preserved; full suite green; ledger and replay artifacts exported.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Corrective context from the previous round:

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: execution.

Intent:
Make a dropped MCP connection invisible to the client: on reconnect, re-attach to the in-flight run and catch up on everything missed, instead of restarting. This is durable-substrate S5, the transport finisher; it consumes S1 read_events_since, S2 idempotent submit / re-attach, and S3a durable terminal outcome. Use app-level resync over existing stdio MCP tools unless implementation evidence forces otherwise. Scope: 1. Add a reconnect/catch-up path: given run_id and last_event_id, return all events since via State.read_events_since; clients advance their cursor exactly once, ascending and gap-tolerant. 2. Re-attach: reconnecting client re-submits idempotently and gets the SAME job back with no duplicate run; poll returns terminal outcome from the ledger if completed during disconnect. 3. Document the reconnect protocol: client persists run_id and last_event_id; on drop -> reconnect -> idempotent re-submit -> catch_up(last_event_id) -> resume polling. 4. Do not rewrite transport or build Streamable HTTP/EventStore in this slice. Non-goals: no S3b projection rebuild, no gate/reviewer changes, no transport rewrite. Acceptance: simulated drop mid-run -> reconnect -> client catches up on all missed events exactly once, re-attaches to same job with no duplicate spawn, and reads terminal outcome from ledger if completed during disconnect; reconnect protocol is covered by an integration test and documented; deterministic replay preserved; full suite green; ledger and replay artifacts exported.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: outcome_review.

Intent:
Make a dropped MCP connection transparent to the client by letting a reconnecting client re-attach to the in-flight run and catch up on missed ledger events instead of restarting. This is durable-substrate S5, the transport finisher; it consumes S1 read_events_since, S2 idempotent submit / re-attach, and S3a durable terminal outcome. Use app-level resync over existing stdio MCP tools, not Streamable HTTP/EventStore. Scope: add catch_up_dual_agent_workflow(run_id, last_event_id, limit) backed by State.read_events_since; prove re-submit with the same client_token returns the same job and does not duplicate launch; prove poll reads the ledger terminal outcome if completion happened during disconnect; document the reconnect protocol. This is a backend protocol and ledger recovery change with no graphical user surface. Acceptance: simulated drop mid-run then reconnect; client catches up on all missed events exactly once, re-attaches to the same job with no duplicate spawn, and reads the terminal outcome from the ledger if completed during disconnect; reconnect protocol is covered by an integration test and documented; deterministic replay preserved; full suite green; ledger and replay artifacts exported.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Corrective context from the previous round:
agents have not both accepted yet; revise and continue

payload.intent: Make a dropped MCP connection transparent to the client by letting a reconnecting client re-attach to the in-flight run and catch up on missed ledger events instead of restarting. This is durable-substrate S5, the transport finisher; it consumes S1 read_events_since, S2 idempotent submit / re-attach, and S3a durable terminal outcome. Use app-level resync over existing stdio MCP tools, not Streamable HTTP/EventStore. Scope: add catch_up_dual_agent_workflow(run_id, last_event_id, limit) backed by State.read_events_since; prove re-submit with the same client_token returns the same job and does not duplicate launch; prove poll reads the ledger terminal outcome if completion happened during disconnect; document the reconnect protocol. This is a backend protocol and ledger recovery change with no graphical user surface. Recovery context: the previous outcome-review text repeated a stale GUI-evidence false positive. The current exported artifact_rigor already shows status ok, user_facing false, missing_artifacts empty, and evidence status not_required. Review the current code, receipts, and current artifact_rigor; do not treat older blocked replay text as a current blocker. Acceptance: simulated drop mid-run then reconnect; client catches up on all missed events exactly once, re-attaches to the same job with no duplicate spawn, and reads the terminal outcome from the ledger if completed during disconnect; reconnect protocol is covered by an integration test and documented; deterministic replay preserved; full suite green; ledger and replay artifacts exported.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: outcome_review.

Intent:
Make a dropped MCP connection transparent to the client by letting a reconnecting client re-attach to the in-flight run and catch up on missed ledger events instead of restarting. This is durable-substrate S5, the transport finisher; it consumes S1 read_events_since, S2 idempotent submit / re-attach, and S3a durable terminal outcome. Use app-level resync over existing stdio MCP tools, not Streamable HTTP/EventStore. Scope: add catch_up_dual_agent_workflow(run_id, last_event_id, limit) backed by State.read_events_since; prove re-submit with the same client_token returns the same job and does not duplicate launch; prove poll reads the ledger terminal outcome if completion happened during disconnect; document the reconnect protocol. This is a backend protocol and ledger recovery change with no graphical user surface. Recovery context: the previous outcome-review text repeated a stale GUI-evidence false positive. The current exported artifact_rigor already shows status ok, user_facing false, missing_artifacts empty, and evidence status not_required. Review the current code, receipts, and current artifact_rigor; do not treat older blocked replay text as a current blocker. Acceptance: simulated drop mid-run then reconnect; client catches up on all missed events exactly once, re-attaches to the same job with no duplicate spawn, and reads the terminal outcome from the ledger if completed during disconnect; reconnect protocol is covered by an integration test and documented; deterministic replay preserved; full suite green; ledger and replay artifacts exported.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

payload.content: S5 resumable-transport implementation verified complete and correct by direct source read: catch_up_dual_agent_workflow is read-only over State.read_events_since with correct non-contiguous cursor semantics; the reconnect integration test proves same-job reattach (no duplicate spawn), exactly-once catch-up, and ledger-first poll after a mid-run drop; reconnect-protocol.md is complete with a doc-presence test.
